institution.name: Geisinger Medical Center
aliases: Geisinger Commonwealth School of Medicine — Visiting Medical Students (covering Geisinger MC + Wyoming Valley + Lewistown placements)

searchTermsTried: Geisinger Medical Center Commonwealth visiting medical student VSLO international elective

candidateFindings.shortQuote: Electives are only open to fourth-year medical students enrolled in medical schools accredited by the Liaison Committee on Medical Education (LCME) or the Commission on Osteopathic College Accreditation (COCA).
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Multispecialty (EM, Ophth, Pain, Ortho, Vascular, Sleep, IM-Peds)

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: Eligibility restricted to LCME/COCA verbatim; international medical schools not eligible.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) US LCME/COCA MS4 only; (b) VSLO required; (c) 2 or 4 week rotations; (d) 3 placement sites (Danville/Wilkes-Barre/Lewistown); (e) cost + visa NOT_STATED.
plainEnglishSummary: Geisinger Medical Center accepts visiting fourth-year medical students through Geisinger Commonwealth SOM's program. Only LCME or COCA-accredited US medical schools eligible. VSLO required. Rotations are 2 or 4 weeks across three Geisinger sites (Danville, Wilkes-Barre, Lewistown). Cost and visa not stated on the hub.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because PA gained a third P101 packet (after UPMC Presbyterian + UPMC Children's); state depth improved.
